description: A discovery catalog of citizen-facing government processes in the United States (US) and the United Kingdom of Great Britain (GB) that an AI agent would commonly carry out on behalf of an individual. Each entry is a candidate for a future GovSchema document, proposed against the registry id convention in registry/README.md. This is a planning artifact, not a registry of published schemas.

scope.audience: Individuals (the 'human companions' an agent acts for). Excludes purely business-internal or government-internal processes except where an individual routinely files them (e.g. sole-trader tax, single-member company formation).
scope.boundary: GovSchema describes a process so an agent can collect and validate the right inputs. It never submits a form, stores anyone's answers, or implies government endorsement. Catalog entries inherit that boundary.

disclaimer: A 'candidate' is a discovery proposal only. It becomes a GovSchema document solely after it is authored against its live government source, validated against spec/v0.1/govschema.schema.json, and given a verification record. 'candidateSource' URLs are starting points to be confirmed at authoring time, not verified sources. Entries with status 'published' link to schemas already in registry/.

tiers.1: High-frequency, well-bounded individual processes with a clear single source form. Strongest next candidates to author.
tiers.2: Common but multi-path, longer, or multi-document processes. Author after tier 1; expect richer flow modelling.
tiers.3: High-value but complex, sensitive, eligibility-heavy, or sub-nationally fragmented (e.g. immigration, means-tested benefits, state-administered programs). Require extra verification rigor and may need several schemas per jurisdiction.

fields.proposedId: Proposed registry id (registry/<id>/<version>/schema.json). Subject to change until authored.
fields.title: Human-readable working title.
fields.jurisdiction: country (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) and level (national | subnational | municipal | supranational).
fields.authority: Government body that owns the process.
fields.processType: One of the spec process.type values: application, registration, renewal, amendment, filing, request, other.
fields.candidateSource: Candidate live source URL to confirm at authoring time.
fields.documentRef: Source form/reference number, where one exists.
fields.agentTask: What an agent would do for the individual.
fields.version: SemVer of the authored schema; present on published entries.
fields.edition: For time-versioned forms only (spec v0.2 §5.7): the edition.label (e.g. a tax year like '2025'). When present, the published schema lives at registry/<proposedId>/<edition>/<version>/schema.json.

candidates.title: United States Passport Renewal by Mail (Form DS-82)

authority.name: U.S. Department of State, Bureau of Consular Affairs

candidates.agentTask: Renew an eligible adult's U.S. passport by mail.
candidates.notes: Reference schema. Already in registry/us/dos/passport-renewal-ds82/1.0.0.

candidates.title: United States First-Time / In-Person Passport Application (Form DS-11)

authority.name: U.S. Department of State, Bureau of Consular Affairs

candidates.agentTask: Prepare a first-time (or in-person required) U.S. passport application.
candidates.notes: Authored in registry/us/dos/passport-application-ds11/1.0.0 (GOV-44). Sibling to the published DS-82 renewal; shares the address/name/photo field cluster. DS-11 must be submitted in person, so the flow ends at an acceptance-facility handoff, not a mail submission. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review.

candidates.title: United States Social Security Card Replacement (Form SS-5)
candidates.agentTask: Request a replacement Social Security card for an individual.
candidates.notes: Authored in registry/us/ssa/social-security-card-replacement-ss5/1.0.0 (GOV-44). Models the online (my Social Security) vs. in-person/by-mail branch; online unavailable for a first card or a legal name change. Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review.

candidates.title: United States Social Security Retirement Benefits Application
candidates.agentTask: Apply for Social Security retirement benefits.
candidates.notes: Eligibility- and date-sensitive (claiming age, work history). Longer flow.

candidates.title: United States Medicare Enrollment

authority.name: U.S. Social Security Administration / Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services

candidates.agentTask: Enroll an individual in Medicare (Parts A/B).
candidates.notes: Enrollment-period rules and Part A/B/C/D branching make this multi-path.

candidates.title: United States Individual Income Tax Return (Form 1040)
candidates.agentTask: Prepare an individual federal income tax return.
candidates.notes: Very high value but large, schedule-driven, and annually versioned (tax year). Likely needs decomposition (1040 core + common schedules) and a tax-year axis in metadata or id.

candidates.title: United States Automatic Extension of Time to File (Form 4868)
candidates.agentTask: Request an automatic extension to file a federal income tax return.
candidates.notes: Reference schema. Authored in registry/us/irs/extension-to-file-4868/2025/1.0.0 (GOV-49). First GovSchema document to exercise the v0.2 edition/tax-year axis (edition.scheme us-tax-year, label 2025); the year is the edition, not part of id or a version bump, and editions coexist (see schema and VERIFICATION.md). Small, well-bounded slice of the IRS surface ahead of the full 1040. Status draft pending full field-by-field source review.

candidates.title: United States Employer Identification Number Application (Form SS-4)
candidates.agentTask: Obtain an EIN for an individual's sole proprietorship or new entity.
candidates.notes: Individual-relevant for sole traders and single-member entities.

candidates.title: United States Application for Naturalization (Form N-400)
candidates.agentTask: Prepare an application for U.S. citizenship.
candidates.notes: High value, sensitive (immigration). Long, eligibility-heavy; demands strict verification.

candidates.title: United States Application to Replace Permanent Resident Card (Form I-90)
candidates.agentTask: Replace or renew a permanent resident card (Green Card).
candidates.notes: More bounded than N-400; good representative USCIS candidate.

candidates.title: United States Alien's Change of Address (Form AR-11)
candidates.agentTask: File a non-citizen's change of address within the required 10 days.
candidates.notes: Authored in registry/us/uscis/change-of-address-ar11/1.0.0 (GOV-44). Short, mandatory, frequent. The 10-day filing rule is a temporal constraint recorded in field descriptions (v0.1 cannot enforce it). Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review.

candidates.title: United States Postal Service Change of Address
candidates.agentTask: Forward an individual's mail after a move.
candidates.notes: Reference schema. Authored in registry/us/usps/change-of-address/1.0.0 (GOV-36). Status draft pending full field-by-field source review.

candidates.title: United States Selective Service Registration
candidates.agentTask: Register an eligible individual with the Selective Service.
candidates.notes: Authored in registry/us/sss/selective-service-registration/1.0.0 (GOV-44). Legally required for most people assigned male at birth ages 18-25; small fixed field set. Age-eligibility window recorded in field descriptions (v0.1 cannot enforce it). Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review.

candidates.title: United States Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA)

authority.name: U.S. Department of Education, Federal Student Aid

candidates.agentTask: Apply for federal student financial aid.
candidates.notes: Annual (award-year axis), long, and dependency/income-driven.

candidates.title: United States VA Health Care Application (Form 10-10EZ)
candidates.agentTask: Apply for VA health care benefits.
candidates.notes: Veteran life-event; eligibility tied to service history.

candidates.title: United States Global Entry (Trusted Traveler) Application
candidates.agentTask: Apply for Global Entry expedited border clearance.
candidates.notes: Includes a conditional interview/appointment step; analogous to GB Registered Traveller.

candidates.title: California Vehicle Registration Renewal
candidates.agentTask: Renew a vehicle's registration in California.
candidates.notes: Reference subnational schema. Already in registry/us/ca/dmv/vehicle-registration-renewal/1.0.0.

candidates.title: California Driver's License Renewal
candidates.agentTask: Renew an individual's California driver's license.
candidates.notes: Authored in registry/us/ca/dmv/drivers-license-renewal/1.0.0 (GOV-44). Pairs with the published CA registration renewal to demonstrate one authority, multiple processes. Models the online/by-mail/in-person branch and the in-person triggers (REAL ID, vision/medical, age). Schema status 'draft' pending full field-by-field source review.

candidates.title: California REAL ID Application
candidates.agentTask: Apply for a federally-compliant REAL ID in California.
candidates.notes: Document-checklist heavy; good exercise for required-evidence modelling.

candidates.title: New York Driver License Renewal

authority.name: New York State Department of Motor Vehicles

candidates.agentTask: Renew an individual's New York driver license.
candidates.notes: Second-state copy of a license renewal — proves the format generalizes across US subdivisions and surfaces cross-state field differences.

candidates.title: California Voter Registration
candidates.agentTask: Register an eligible individual to vote in California.
candidates.notes: Voter registration is state-administered in the US; the federal vote.gov entry only routes to state systems. Model per-state, not nationally.

candidates.title: California LLC Formation (Articles of Organization, Form LLC-1)
candidates.agentTask: Form a single-member LLC in California for an individual.
candidates.notes: Company formation is state-administered in the US; pairs with GB Companies House to show the same process across jurisdictions.

candidates.title: United States SNAP (Food Assistance) Application

authority.name: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Food and Nutrition Service

candidates.agentTask: Apply for SNAP (food) benefits.
candidates.notes: Federally funded but state-administered: each state has its own application. The national entry is informational; real schemas live at subnational level (e.g. us/ca/...). Flagged here so the fragmentation is recorded, not assumed away.

candidates.title: United Kingdom Adult Passport Renewal
candidates.agentTask: Renew an adult UK passport.
candidates.notes: Reference schema. Already in registry/gb/hmpo/passport-renewal-adult/1.0.0.

candidates.title: United Kingdom First Adult Passport Application
candidates.agentTask: Apply for a first UK adult passport.
candidates.notes: Sibling to the published renewal; adds first-time identity evidence (countersignatory, documents).

candidates.title: United Kingdom Photocard Driving Licence Renewal
candidates.agentTask: Renew a 10-year photocard driving licence.
candidates.notes: Exemplar-service authority (DVLA). Bounded, high-frequency.

candidates.title: United Kingdom First Provisional Driving Licence
candidates.agentTask: Apply for a first provisional driving licence.
candidates.notes: Common young-adult life-event; identity-document driven.

candidates.title: United Kingdom Vehicle Tax (Vehicle Excise Duty)
candidates.agentTask: Tax a vehicle (or set up the Direct Debit) on behalf of the keeper.
candidates.notes: Keyed off a reference number (V5C/V11/V85). Small, recurring.

candidates.title: United Kingdom Change of Vehicle Keeper (V5C)
candidates.agentTask: Notify DVLA that a vehicle was sold or bought.
candidates.notes: Exemplar #11 (register a vehicle) family.

candidates.title: United Kingdom Self Assessment Tax Return (SA100)
candidates.agentTask: File an individual's Self Assessment tax return.
candidates.notes: GB counterpart to IRS 1040: high value, supplementary-page driven, tax-year axis. Exemplar #16 (pay your tax).

candidates.title: United Kingdom National Insurance Number Application

authority.name: His Majesty's Revenue and Customs / Department for Work and Pensions

candidates.agentTask: Apply for a National Insurance number.
candidates.notes: Identity-verification driven; common for new arrivals and school-leavers.

candidates.title: United Kingdom Child Benefit Claim (CH2)
candidates.agentTask: Claim Child Benefit for a new child.
candidates.notes: Child Benefit is HMRC-administered (not DWP). High-charge income threshold adds a conditional branch.

candidates.title: United Kingdom Marriage Allowance Transfer
candidates.agentTask: Transfer part of a personal allowance between spouses/civil partners.
candidates.notes: Small, eligibility-gated; a clean tier-1 HMRC slice.

candidates.title: United Kingdom Universal Credit Claim
candidates.agentTask: Make a Universal Credit claim.
candidates.notes: Exemplar #14. Means-tested, long, circumstance-driven; sensitive. Strict verification.

candidates.title: United Kingdom State Pension Claim
candidates.agentTask: Claim the State Pension.
candidates.notes: Triggered by reaching State Pension age; bounded inputs.

candidates.title: United Kingdom Personal Independence Payment (PIP)
candidates.agentTask: Start a Personal Independence Payment claim.
candidates.notes: Exemplar #13. Health/disability data — highly sensitive; assessment-driven.

candidates.title: United Kingdom Carer's Allowance
candidates.agentTask: Claim Carer's Allowance.
candidates.notes: Exemplar #12. Eligibility tied to the cared-for person's benefits.

candidates.title: United Kingdom Register to Vote
candidates.agentTask: Register an individual to vote in the UK.
candidates.notes: Exemplar #1. Single nationwide service; bounded fields (name, address, NINo, nationality).

candidates.title: United Kingdom Private Limited Company Incorporation (IN01)
candidates.agentTask: Incorporate a private limited company for an individual.
candidates.notes: GB counterpart to US state LLC formation; nationwide single authority, unlike the US.

candidates.title: United Kingdom Standard Visitor Visa Application
candidates.agentTask: Apply for a UK Standard Visitor visa.
candidates.notes: Exemplar #21. Immigration — sensitive, document-heavy, applicant-nationality conditional.

candidates.title: United Kingdom Lasting Power of Attorney
candidates.agentTask: Make and register a Lasting Power of Attorney.
candidates.notes: Exemplar #25. Multi-party (donor, attorneys, certificate provider, witnesses); legally weighty.

candidates.title: United Kingdom Register with a GP (GMS1)
candidates.agentTask: Register an individual with an NHS GP surgery.
candidates.notes: Health is devolved (England/Scotland/Wales/NI differ). Start with England GMS1; record the devolution split in jurisdiction notes.
